headline: Final set: Spain face Argentina on 19 July at New York New Jersey Stadium after Spain beat France 2–0 and Argentina came from behind 2–1 vs England.

snapshot.format: 48 teams · 12 groups of 4 · Round of 32 → Round of 16 → Quarter-finals → Semi-finals → Final
snapshot.opening_match_note: First finals with three host nations and a Round of 32 knockout round

firsts: First World Cup co-hosted by three nations
First World Cup with a Round of 32 stage

hosts_detail.note: First time Canada hosts men's World Cup matches — exited Round of 16 to Morocco
Mexico becomes the first nation to host World Cup matches in three different editions (1970, 1986, 2026) — exited Round of 16 to England
Final staged at New York New Jersey Stadium (MetLife) — USMNT exited Round of 16 to Belgium

fun_facts.title: Triple hosts
fun_facts.fact: FIFA World Cup 2026 is the first men's World Cup co-hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico, and the United States.

fun_facts.title: Mexico's historic three
fun_facts.fact: Mexico is the first nation to stage World Cup matches across three different tournaments (1970, 1986, and 2026).

fun_facts.title: Expanded field
fun_facts.fact: The 2026 finals expanded to 48 teams in 12 groups of four — the largest World Cup field in history.

fun_facts.title: New knockout round
fun_facts.fact: 2026 introduced a Round of 32 between the group stage and the Round of 16 for the first time at a men's World Cup.

fun_facts.title: Finalists locked in
fun_facts.fact: Spain and Argentina will contest the 2026 World Cup Final on 19 July at New York New Jersey Stadium.

fun_facts.title: Spain outplay France
fun_facts.fact: Spain beat France 2–0 in the semi-final at Dallas Stadium (Oyarzabal pen, Porro) to reach the 2026 Final.

fun_facts.title: Argentina stun England
fun_facts.fact: Argentina came from behind to beat England 2–1 in Atlanta — Enzo Fernández equalised late and Lautaro Martínez headed the stoppage-time winner.

fun_facts.title: All three hosts exit R16
fun_facts.fact: Canada, Mexico, and the United States were all eliminated in the Round of 16 (to Morocco, England, and Belgium respectively).

fun_facts.title: Messi leads Golden Boot
fun_facts.fact: Lionel Messi leads the Golden Boot on assists tiebreaker — 8 goals and 4 assists vs Mbappé's 8 goals and 3 assists — heading into the Final.

fun_facts.title: Bronze in Miami
fun_facts.fact: England face France in the third-place match on 18 July at Miami Stadium.

fun_facts.title: Holders chase history
fun_facts.fact: Argentina are aiming for back-to-back World Cup titles after Qatar 2022, facing Spain in the 2026 Final.

fun_facts.title: Spain seek a second star
fun_facts.fact: Spain return to a World Cup Final for the first time since winning in 2010, after dominating France in the 2026 semi-final.

fun_facts.title: Final venue
fun_facts.fact: The 2026 World Cup Final is at New York New Jersey Stadium (MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford) on 19 July 2026.

fun_facts.title: Haaland on seven
fun_facts.fact: Erling Haaland finished with 7 goals for Norway, third in the Golden Boot race after Norway’s quarter-final exit.

fun_facts.title: Sixteen host cities
fun_facts.fact: Matches at World Cup 2026 are staged across 16 host cities in Canada, Mexico, and the United States.

fun_facts.title: Goals flowing
fun_facts.fact: World Cup 2026 has remained high-scoring through the knockouts, averaging about 2.9 goals per match into the final weekend.

tournament_so_far.story: The first 48-team World Cup reached its final weekend with Spain and Argentina booked for New York New Jersey Stadium. Spain controlled France 2–0 in Dallas; Argentina produced another late turnaround to beat England 2–1 in Atlanta. All three co-hosts exited in the Round of 16. Messi leads the Golden Boot on assists with the Final and bronze-medal match still to come.
highlights: Final: Spain vs Argentina — 19 July, New York New Jersey Stadium
Third place: England vs France — 18 July, Miami Stadium
Argentina 2–1 England (SF) — Fernández, Martínez late comeback
All three hosts (Canada, Mexico, USA) exited Round of 16
Messi leads Golden Boot 8G/4A over Mbappé 8G/3A

sources: FIFA / Sky Sports / Yahoo Sports coverage (July 2026)
disclaimer: Arena64 snapshot for agent knowledge & coaches — refreshed by the World Cup Monitor from open-web football coverage. Not an official FIFA live feed.
